data.title: Wendy's plans 60 new restaurants in Mexico through partnerships
data.description:
Wendy’s (WEN) is furthering its expansion efforts in Mexico with plans for more than 60 new restaurants in the country through partnerships with AJ Group and WS Pacific, capitalizing on the popularity of quick-service hamburger restaurants. 

Wendy’s (WEN) currently operates 40 restaurants across Mexico, including locations in Ciudad Juarez, Chihuahua, Monterrey, and Mexico City.

The burger chain will establish 50 new restaurants in Mexico City, Hidalgo, and Morelos with AJ Group, with the Mexico City restaurant expected to open this year.

In the northern part of the country, Wendy’s (WEN) and WS Pacific will develop 12 new restaurants across the states of Sinaloa and Durango, with the first restaurant expected to open later this year.

Wendy’s long-term goal targets 400 restaurants across the country.

By comparison, McDonald’s (MCD) operates 374 restaurants, while Burger King (QSR) operates 435 restaurants across Mexico.

data.title: Ex-OpenAI research chief seeks $70M for AI factory automation startup: WSJ
data.description:
Bob McGrew, the former chief research officer at OpenAI (OPENAI), is raising about $70 million for a new artificial intelligence startup focused on transforming manufacturing, The Wall Street Journal reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the effort.

The company, called Arda, is seeking funding at a valuation of roughly $700 million. Venture firms Founders Fund and Accel are leading the round, with Khosla Ventures and XYZ Venture Capital also participating. The financing hasn’t yet closed and the final terms could still change, according to the report.

Arda’s name references the mythical world created by J.R.R. Tolkien in “The Lord of the Rings,” reflecting the company’s ambition to bridge artificial intelligence with real-world industrial operations.

The startup is developing a software and AI platform designed to help factories operate more autonomously. One component includes a video-based AI model that can study footage from production floors and use those insights to train robotic systems to carry out manufacturing tasks.

The broader platform aims to coordinate machines and human workers throughout the entire production cycle. That includes early design stages, manufacturing feasibility and the final stages of goods moving off assembly lines.

Arda’s founders hope the technology could help make manufacturing more economically viable in Western countries, at a time when companies and governments are increasingly focused on reducing dependence on Chinese supply chains for geopolitical and national security reasons.

McGrew spent eight years at OpenAI before leaving in 2024. During his tenure he led research efforts aimed at teaching robots to perform tasks in physical environments, according to his LinkedIn profile. Earlier in his career he was among the first employees at data analytics company Palantir (PLTR).

He is launching Arda with Augustus Odena, a former co-founder of Adept AI, along with Palantir veterans Jakob Frick and Alex Mark, the Journal reported.

data.title: Bitcoin tops $73K and hits a fresh one-month high as momentum builds
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) advanced sharply on Wednesday, climbing to its highest level in roughly a month as renewed momentum lifted the world’s largest cryptocurrency above the $73,000 mark. 

The digital asset briefly touched an intraday peak of $73,546, representing a gain of about 6.8% on the session and one of its strongest single-day moves in recent weeks. 

The rally pushed bitcoin to levels not seen since early February, signaling a rebound after a period of choppy trading. Market participants have been watching for signs that the cryptocurrency could regain upward momentum following a pullback earlier in the year. 

Despite the latest surge, bitcoin remains below several widely followed technical indicators, including its 50-day, 100-day, and 200-day moving averages. Those levels are often viewed by traders as key gauges of longer-term trend strength, suggesting the broader technical picture remains mixed even as prices recover. 

Bitcoin ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).

Blockchain ETFs: (BITQ), (DAPP), (BKCH), (BLOK), (CRPT), and (IBLC).

data.title: Hexcel reaches agreement with Vision One Fund, adds Neal Keating to board
data.description:
Hexcel (HXL) reached a cooperation agreement with activist investor Vision One Fund that will add a new independent director to the aerospace materials company’s board while ending a potential proxy fight, according to a regulatory filing. Hexcel's (HXL) shares were little changed on Wednesday.

Under the agreement, Hexcel appointed Neal J. Keating to its board of directors and to the board’s audit committee, effective March 3. The company also agreed to nominate Keating for election at its 2026 annual meeting of stockholders.

The agreement was reached with Vision One Fund and affiliated entities, which together own about 640,329 shares of Hexcel’s (HXL) common stock.

As part of the deal, Vision One Fund agreed to withdraw its previously submitted nominations for board seats at the 2026 annual meeting and to comply with customary standstill provisions. These include restrictions on proxy solicitations and commitments to support the company’s slate of director nominees through the term of the agreement.

The cooperation agreement also limits the size of Hexcel’s (HXL) board to no more than 10 directors until the 2026 annual meeting and nine directors afterward, unless the Vision One parties consent to a larger board.

Keating, 70, previously served as chairman, president and chief executive officer of aerospace supplier Kaman from 2008 to 2020 and later as executive chairman until 2021. He has also held leadership roles at Hughes Supply and GKN Aerospace and currently serves on the board of Hubbell.

The agreement will remain in effect until shortly before the nomination deadline for the company’s 2027 annual meeting or roughly one year after the 2026 annual meeting, whichever occurs earlier.

data.title: Marvell Q4 Preview: Analysts divided ahead of earnings, data centre & AI momentum in focus
data.description:
Marvell (MRVL) is projected to report a healthy Q4 print on March 5, after the closing bell.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $0.79, representing about 32% year-on-year jump, while revenue is expected to jump 21.4% to $2.21B, in line with the management’s revenue guidance of $2.2B at the midpoint.

Seeking Alpha analyst Dair Sansyzbayev noted that analysts’ optimism is driven by recent developments around the global AI infrastructure buildout and the company's growth endeavors.

“Marvell's management is working hard to fortify the company's market positioning in the AI era. The company's growth strategy remains comprehensive, including both organic and inorganic growth,” he added. The analyst was bullish on the stock calling it a "compelling buy".However, another SA analyst Forward Analytics was bearish on the stock ahead of earnings anticipating easing growth momentum and a normalization phase following earlier explosive gains.

It urged investors must keep a close eye on data centre & AI momentum, the company’s key growth engine. Any commentary or updates on accelerating AI CapEx from cloud giants provide a major growth catalyst, it said. 

FY27 guidance is another key area of focus, Forward Analytics added.

"In my view, any revenue guidance in the region of 20%-35% would be within my long-term expected growth rate, and this would potentially mark the end of the stock’s volatility since growth could stabilize within that region," it noted.

Last quarter, the management had guided data center revenue growth of more than 25% Y/Y in FY27.

Over the last 2 years, MRVL has beaten EPS as well as revenue estimates 75% of the time, raising investors' hopes of another solid quarter. The stock, however, has underperformed the wider markets in the year so far, declining more than 8%.

data.title: Brazil court blocks Equinox Gold mineral rights transfer, jeopardizing asset sale
data.description:
A Brazilian court blocked Equinox Gold (EQX) from transferring mineral rights for gold mines it sold to ​Chinese metals miner and processor CMOC Group, Reuters reported late Tuesday, casting uncertainty over a transaction valued at slightly more than $1B.

The ruling followed complaints from local state-run firm CBPM, which leases ‌the ⁠mineral assets and said the deal breaks its leasing contract, as the decision shows "the ​irregularity ⁠in the negotiation of a mineral asset belonging to the people of Bahia state."

In January, Equinox (EQX) completed the sale of its Aurizona mine, RDM mine, and Bahia complex in Brazil to CMOC Group, saying it received cash proceeds of $900M, and will receive a production linked contingent cash payment of up to $115M in January 2027.

Despite the legal uncertainty, the company reaffirmed its FY 2026 production guidance of 700K-800K oz of gold.

data.title: Fed's Miran still sees rate cuts as appropriate amid Iran war
data.description:
The development of the war in Iran hasn't led Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran to change his mind about supporting further easing of the federal funds rate, he said on Wednesday in a televised interview.

He said he would consider pausing rate cuts if he saw concerns about higher inflation in the markets. "I don't get the impression the market is concerned about long-term inflation expectations," he said during an interview on Bloomberg Television.

Miran supports resuming rate cuts by 25 basis points per meeting.

"I think it's a moment to continue acting," he said. "Thus far, the evidence from events over the weekend haven't led me to change my forecasts for the labor market or inflation over the medium term. So it's too early to respond to them."

The Federal Governor, who had headed the White House Council of Economic Advisers, still views the federal funds rate as “modestly restrictive” and figures the rate stands at about 1 percentage point above neutral — the point at which the interest rate neither fuels nor hinders economic growth. "My view is we ought to start getting back towards neutral," Miran said.

Short-run inflation expectations have come up "quite a bit when you look at CPI swaps," he said. "But that's just because of a mechanical read-through of oil prices into headline inflation."

"At the end of the day, I don't see an inflation problem in the United States," he added.

Many of Miran's colleagues on the Federal Open Market Committee are more willing to wait and see how the war affects the U.S. economy before cutting any further. On Tuesday, Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari said it's too early to tell what the conflict's impact will be on inflation. And Kansas City Fed President Jeffrey Schmid is still focused on inflation running too hot. 

Traders, though, don't expect the central bank to cut its key rate until the summer. Rate traders see a 59.7% probability that the rate will stay unchanged through the Federal Open Market Committee's June 16-17 meeting, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

data.title: Bayer falls as 2026 outlook misses consensus
data.description:
Bayer (BAYRY) dropped ~6% on Wednesday after the German conglomerate projected adjusted earnings that fell short of average analysts’ estimates for 2026 as the company eyes a multibillion-dollar settlement to resolve cancer claims related to its Roundup weedkiller.

The life sciences firm estimated €9.1B - €9.6B ($10.6B - $11.2B) of adjusted EBITDA for this year, slightly missing the €9.67B projected by analysts, according to a company-provided consensus posted on its website.

For 2025, Bayer (BAYZF) reported €9.7B in adjusted EBITDA on €45.6B in sales, indicating a ~5% YoY drop and a ~1.1% YoY growth on a currency- and portfolio-adjusted basis, respectively. Its agriculture business added €21.6B to the topline with ~1% YoY growth, while sales from its self-care products remained flat at €5.8B.

However, the Pharmaceuticals division grew ~2% YoY, generating €17.8B to the topline, thanks mainly to sales from cancer therapy Nubeqa and renal drug Kerendia, which showed 62% YoY and 88% YoY growth, respectively.

Meanwhile, sales from blood thinner Xarelto, marketed with J&J (JNJ), fell ~32% YoY due to loss of market exclusivity, and sales from eyecare therapy Eylea, marketed with Regeneron (REGN), dropped ~4% YoY amid lower prices and rivalry from off-patent versions.

Bayer (BAYZF) projected €4.00 - €4.50 in core earnings per share for 2026 on €44B - €46B in group revenue.

Morgan Stanley said that the guidance looked "decent," but it was below Street forecasts. “Investors will continue to focus on the glyphosate litigation, which will drive the share price in the coming weeks and months,” analysts led by Thibault Boutherin wrote, according to Bloomberg.

Last month, Bayer (BAYRY) agreed to pay more than $7B as part of a settlement to resolve current and future cancer cases linked to Roundup, added to the company with its Monsanto takeover for more than $60B in 2018.

data.title: U.S. LNG plants running near full capacity, limiting potential to replace Qatar disruptions
data.description:
Even as the world's largest liquefied natural gas producer, the U.S. has little spare capacity to quickly raise LNG output to offset lost supply after Qatar stopped shipments, Reuters reported Wednesday.

The U.S. exports nearly 19B cf/day of natural gas that is converted into LNG, nearly double the 10B cf/day Qatar removed from the market after the wave of attacks on Iran, but domestic export plants are running nearly full tilt with most cargoes locked into long‑term contracts.

Top U.S. exporter Cheniere Energy (LNG) sold 46M metric tons of LNG last year and on Tuesday was drawing more than 7B cf/day of feed gas for its two Gulf Coast terminals; the company recently began production from Train 5 of its Stage 3 ⁠expansion at Corpus Christi, the unit - with capacity to produce a relatively small 1.5M tons/year - is expected to take about a month to reach full output, most of which has been contracted out.

Second-ranked U.S. producer Venture Global (VG) has the most flexibility to help in ​the short term because it is selling as much as 4B cf/day of commissioning volumes from its Plaquemines plant in Louisiana on the spot market, which allows the company more room to redirect cargoes, CEO Mike Sabel said this week on an earnings conference call; Plaquemines will be capable of producing 35M tons/year when fully online.

The Golden Pass LNG project, a joint venture between QatarEnergy and Exxon ‌Mobil (XOM), is expected to ⁠begin initial production this month, with demand for gas to be converted into LNG expected to reach ~800M cf/day for the first 6M tons/year plant.

"With the U.S. already exporting LNG at effectively maximum capacity, the war cannot materially increase physical demand for U.S. gas in the short‑ to medium‑term," EBW Analytics said in a note.
